description: One-pass review of crate secretx-k8s (secretx-k8s). Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
Decision: forbidden (403) is permanent (RBAC denied), not transient. Mapping it to Unavailable will cause callers to retry-loop on permission denial. Conversely, transient kube errors (HyperError/Service/RustlsTls/timeouts/5xx/429) currently fall through to Backend (permanent) and will not be retried. Filing as P0 correctness.
Decision: filing race-on-create (PATCH 404 -> POST AlreadyExists) as P1 risk; the AlreadyExists path returns Backend (permanent) which is wrong — caller's retry policy will not recover.
Decision: tests do not assert outgoing request shape (URL, method, body, field-manager). Mock pattern receives (_, send) and discards request. Filing as P1 test-gap; SSA could be silently broken without test signal.
Decision: filing third-copy data.clone() on patch path as P1 risk (plaintext zeroization gap widening). The first copy is unavoidable per existing comment; the third is not.
Maintainer risk: HIGH — error mapping is inverted (issue 1) which will cause infinite-retry on permission denial and immediate failure on transient outages. Concurrent put creates can fail with confusing AlreadyExists (issue 2). Tests do not assert request shape (issue 5) so SSA could regress silently.
Correctness confidence: 70% chance of a missed bug. Least confident flags: SSA fallthrough on 404 (issue 3) — the original author's intent may capture a real edge case I cannot reproduce; data.clone() third-copy quantification (issue 4) — depends on whether reqwest serialization truly drops the K8sSecret synchronously. Uncertainty: behavior under apiserver mid-write rolling restarts; behavior with admission webhooks that mutate Secrets; behavior across kube 3.x minor version bumps.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — the crate is idiomatic Rust at the level a serious practitioner reads without wincing. The real findings are correctness (Pass 1), not idioms. Most idiom-pass flags (.21, .22, .23, .24) are opinion-tagged because there is no engineering basis to change them.
Rust idiom confidence: 85% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: none — idiom surface is small and well-contained. Uncertainty: I did not run cargo clippy --all-features (review-only rules forbid no edits but allow read-only invocation; chose not to in the interest of finishing the pass cleanly given the heavier correctness findings).
title: Review: secretx-k8s rusty pass
